raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"The draft's core framing — 'first method for viewpoint-shift generalization' and the deployment-gap contribution claim — are load-bearing assertions that any improvement pass must either preserve exactly or substantively revise; they cannot be touched incidentally.","R1.2":"A material logical tension exists: the paper is framed around cross-viewpoint generalization yet all reported evidence (Cityscapes-val, BDD100K-val) uses standard single-viewpoint splits, leaving the central claim under-supported; improvement work that ignores this risks compounding the inconsistency.","R1.3":"Six specific quantitative claims are embedded in the text (78.4 mIoU, +1.6, +1.2, −0.9, ±8°, ±5°); any rewrite must preserve accurate alignment between these numbers and what the described experiments actually show.","R1.6":"'Improve this draft' at minimum loads prose and sentence-level quality; this dimension is unambiguously in scope regardless of how the substantive-content question resolves."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should I limit improvements to prose and sentence-level polish only, or also address substantive content issues — specifically whether the standard-benchmark results adequately support the viewpoint-generalization claim and whether the 'first method' novelty assertion is defensible?"}

rationale.R1.1: The draft's core framing — 'first method for viewpoint-shift generalization' and the deployment-gap contribution claim — are load-bearing assertions that any improvement pass must either preserve exactly or substantively revise; they cannot be touched incidentally.
rationale.R1.2: A material logical tension exists: the paper is framed around cross-viewpoint generalization yet all reported evidence (Cityscapes-val, BDD100K-val) uses standard single-viewpoint splits, leaving the central claim under-supported; improvement work that ignores this risks compounding the inconsistency.
rationale.R1.3: Six specific quantitative claims are embedded in the text (78.4 mIoU, +1.6, +1.2, −0.9, ±8°, ±5°); any rewrite must preserve accurate alignment between these numbers and what the described experiments actually show.
rationale.R1.6: 'Improve this draft' at minimum loads prose and sentence-level quality; this dimension is unambiguously in scope regardless of how the substantive-content question resolves.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I limit improvements to prose and sentence-level polish only, or also address substantive content issues — specifically whether the standard-benchmark results adequately support the viewpoint-generalization claim and whether the 'first method' novelty assertion is defensible?
